problems.title: Count Occurrences of All Elements
problems.description:
In this problem, you are required to count the number of occurrences (frequency) of each element in the array.

You will be given an array of integers. Your task is to print each unique element along with how many times it appears.

In Java, this can be efficiently solved using a HashMap where the key is the element and the value is its frequency.

This problem is important for understanding frequency counting and is widely used in problems involving hashing and data analysis.
problems.input_format:
First line contains integer n.
Second line contains n integers.
problems.output_format: Print each element and its frequency (element:count).

sample_tests.explanation: 1 appears twice, 2 appears twice, 3 appears once.

problems.title: Replace Negative Numbers with Zero
problems.description:
In this problem, you need to replace all negative numbers in an array with zero.

You will be given an array of integers. Traverse the array and whenever you encounter a negative number, replace it with 0.

This problem helps in understanding array modification and conditional updates.
problems.input_format:
First line contains integer n.
Second line contains n integers.

problems.title: Multiply All Elements
problems.description:
In this problem, you are required to compute the product of all elements in an array.

You will be given an array of integers. Multiply all elements and print the result.

Be careful with large numbers as the result may grow quickly. You can assume the result fits within a 64-bit integer.

This problem helps in understanding cumulative operations similar to sum but with multiplication.
problems.input_format:
First line contains integer n.
Second line contains n integers.

problems.title: Find Average of Array
problems.description:
In this problem, you need to calculate the average (mean) of all elements in an array.

The average is calculated as sum of all elements divided by the number of elements.

In Java, compute the sum first and then divide by n. Make sure to use appropriate data types to avoid integer division issues.
problems.input_format:
First line contains integer n.
Second line contains n integers.

problems.title: Find Range of Array
problems.description:
In this problem, you are required to find the range of the array.

The range is defined as the difference between the maximum and minimum elements (range = max - min).

You should first find the maximum and minimum values in the array, then compute their difference.

This problem helps combine multiple operations in a single traversal.
problems.input_format:
First line contains integer n.
Second line contains n integers.
